name: Penne with Vodka and Spicy-Tomato Cream Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon Crushed red pepper, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In an unheated skillet large enough to hold the pasta later on, combine oil, garlic, crushed red pepper, and a pinch of salt. Stir until well coated.
2. Cook over moderate heat just until the garlic turns golden but not brown, 2 to 3 minutes.
3. If using whole canned tomatoes, place a food mill over the skillet and puree the tomatoes directly into it. Crushed tomatoes can be added directly and stirred to blend.
4. Simmer uncovered until the sauce begins to thicken, about 15 minutes. Taste for seasoning.
5. Meanwhile, in a large pot, bring 6 quarts of water to a rolling boil. Add 3 tablespoons salt and the penne, stirring to prevent sticking. Cook until tender but firm to the bite, then drain thoroughly.
6. Add the drained pasta to the skillet with the tomato sauce. Toss well.
7. Add the vodka, toss again, then add the cream and toss once more.
8. Cover, reduce heat to low for 1 to 2 minutes for the pasta to absorb the sauce.
9. Add parsley and toss again. Transfer to warmed shallow soup bowls and serve immediately. (Traditionally, cheese is not served with this dish.)
